entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A food legume used as a source of dietary proteins in several countries.

entities.name: PROTEIN QUALITY
entities.description: The nutritional value of proteins in pigeonpea, influenced by amino acid composition, anti-nutritional factors, and processing.

entities.name: SULPHUR AMINO ACIDS
entities.description: Amino acids containing sulphur, such as methionine and cystine, which are limiting in pigeonpea.

entities.name: METHIONINE
entities.description: A limiting sulphur amino acid in pigeonpea.

entities.name: CYSTINE
entities.description: A limiting sulphur amino acid in pigeonpea.

entities.name: PROTEASE INHIBITORS
entities.description: Compounds that inhibit protease enzymes, with concentrations varying among pigeonpea varieties and wild relatives.

entities.name: WILD RELATIVES OF PIGEONPEA
entities.description: Natural relatives of the cultivated pigeonpea that often contain higher levels of protease inhibitors.

entities.name: PROTEIN DIGESTIBILITY
entities.description: The extent to which proteins in cooked pigeonpea meal are digested, which is influenced by various compounds.

entities.name: TRYPSIN INHIBITORS
entities.description: A type of protease inhibitor that can negatively impact protein digestibility.

entities.name: POLYPHENOLIC COMPOUNDS
entities.description: Compounds in pigeonpea that inhibit the activity of digestive enzymes.

entities.name: DIGESTIVE ENZYMES
entities.description: Enzymes responsible for protein digestion, whose activity is affected by polyphenolic compounds.

entities.name: STORAGE PRACTICES
entities.description: The methods of storing pigeonpea that influence its protein quality.

entities.name: PROCESSING PRACTICES
entities.description: The methods of processing pigeonpea that influence its protein quality.
